concepts.name: Singly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where each element points to the next element.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: The basic building block of a linked list, containing data and a pointer to the next node.

concepts.name: Head Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A reference to the first node in a linked list.

concepts.name: Tail Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A reference to the last node in a linked list.

concepts.name: Iterative Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting each node in a linked list sequentially using a loop.

concepts.name: Pointer Manipulation
concepts.short_definition: Modifying the 'next' references between nodes to alter the list structure.

concepts.name: Merging Sorted Lists
concepts.short_definition: Combining two or more sorted linked lists into a single sorted linked list.

concepts.name: Dummy Node
concepts.short_definition: A temporary node often used to simplify the logic of list manipulation, especially at the head.

concepts.name: Comparison of Node Values
concepts.short_definition: Determining the relative order of elements in linked lists based on their data.

concepts.name: Recursive Merging
concepts.short_definition: A method of merging linked lists by breaking the problem into smaller, self-similar subproblems.
